kept.text:
Charlotte’s Brain Tumour Journey
18.2.1997 – 24.2.2016
Diagnosis to death: 951 days
Age 19 years 5 days

During  May half-term, a colleague and his wife will be taking on an incredible challenge walking the Camino Português from Ponte de Lima to Santiago de Compostela. Covering around 140 km in just seven days, they’ll be raising both funds and awareness for Charlotte’s BAG along the way.

We’re especially grateful to Dani’s employer, @Intuit, who will be matching all donations. This means every contribution will have double the impact.

Charlotte’s BAG is a completely self-funding charity:
No salaries
No overheads
No advertising
No merchandise
We even personally cover PayPal and PO Box fees, ensuring that every penny donated goes directly Charlotte's Lab, Kings College Hospital, London.
Thank you so much for your support  it truly makes a difference. 
Would be so grateful for a RT TY
https://t.co/Z4OMVb32Sk

profile_bio.description: Brain Cancer took my daughter Feb 2016 age 19 yrs raising awareness by sharing her story & funding 4 research @ CLAB https://t.co/oMU6LrOIqf

kept.text:
🇷🇺🛢️🔥 𝗢𝗶𝗹 𝗦𝗮𝗻𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀... 𝗨𝗸𝗿𝗮𝗶𝗻𝗶𝗮𝗻-𝘀𝘁𝘆𝗹𝗲!

Ukrainian forces struck the sanctioned Cameroonian-flagged VLCC MARQUISE (IMO: 9315745) approximately 120 NM SW of Tuapse. 

According to the AFU General Staff, the vessel was adrift and potentially waiting to enter port in Tuapse once the security situation improved or waiting for another tanker to perform an at-sea transfer.

The AFU did not specify the means of the strike but it is likely they used a USV (Magura or similar).

Coincidentally, a NATO (Italian) RQ-4D Phoenix ISR UAS, based out of Sigonella, was operating over the Black Sea in the general area of the MARQUISE.

#OSINT #RussianBloodOil

kept.text:
🇷🇺🛢️🔥 𝗢𝗶𝗹 𝗦𝗮𝗻𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀 𝗨𝗸𝗿𝗮𝗶𝗻𝗶𝗮𝗻-𝘀𝘁𝘆𝗹𝗲 | 𝗪𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗥𝘂𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗮𝗻 𝗔𝗶𝗿 𝗗𝗲𝗳𝗲𝗻𝘀𝗲𝘀 𝗗𝗼𝗶𝗻𝗴?

Ukrainian SBU long-range drone teams conducted an overnight strike on the Linear Production and Dispatch Station (LPDS) Perm which is about 1,500 km (930 miles) from Ukraine.

LPDS Perm, owned by PJSC Transneft, is the largest oil pumping station in the Perm Regional Oil Pipeline Management System and serves as the key dispatch hub.

The station's centrifugal mainline pumps have a capacity of 10,000 m^3/h which is equivalent to moving 140 rail tanker cars (around 60,000 bbls) of oil every hour.

Recent months have seen a notable increase in successful Ukrainian long-range drone strikes on Russian oil infrastructure, including refineries, export terminals, and pumping stations. These operations highlight chronic strains on Russia's air defense network, which must cover an immense territory. 

Ukraine's continued scaling of long-range drone production has enabled deeper and more frequent attacks, complicating Russia's ability to position sufficient SAM systems and radars everywhere. In addition, Ukraine's development of intermediate-range strike drones with FPV capabilities has significantly increased the attrition rate of hard-to-replace Russian SAM systems and radars. 

Location: 57.799420, 55.885536

Sources: AFU General Staff | Open Source Videos | https://t.co/4FC0AA2kub

#OSINT #RussianBloodOil

kept.text:
𝗥𝘂𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗮'𝘀 𝗩𝗶𝗰𝘁𝗼𝗿𝘆 𝗗𝗮𝘆 𝗣𝗮𝗿𝗮𝗱𝗲 | 𝗡𝗼 𝗘𝗾𝘂𝗶𝗽𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁, 𝗡𝗼 𝗖𝗮𝗱𝗲𝘁𝘀

For the first time since 2008, Russia's May 9 Victory Day parade in Moscow will roll without a single tank, armored vehicle, or missile system on Red Square. 

Last year's parade featured over 11,500 troops, 180-plus vehicles, Yars ICBM launchers, and battlefield equipment pulled directly from Ukraine. Xi Jinping was in the front row.

The Russian Ministry of Defense cited the "current operational situation." No further explanation was offered.

This year, Suvorov and Nakhimov military boarding school cadets will be absent. Officer candidates from MoD military academies will march in their place.

All rolling-stock hardware stays home.

The Russian Knights will not fly. 

On May 7, 2025, two days before last year's parade, Ukrainian drones struck Kubinka Air Base, home of the Knights and their Su-30SM and Su-35S fleet, igniting fires along the flight line. 

The Kremlin is apparently not interested in a repeat. Instead, the MoD announced that "pilots of Su-25 ground attack aircraft will color the skies of Moscow in the colors of the flag of the Russian Federation."

This year, the Kremlin has not released a list of invited foreign leaders. If the Kremlin had published a list of invitees, the empty chairs this year, compared to 2025, would tell a story about what a rough 12-months Russia's closest allies had in the interim. 

The "current operational situation" is doing a lot of work in that statement.

Sources: Russian Ministry of Defense | RIA Novosti | Moscow Times 

#OSINT #Ukraine
